calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Joinder Or, In The Alternative, To Consolidate Cases
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HYRECAR INC.'S Motion To Compel Joinder Or, In The Alternative, To Consolidate Cases.

Defendant Hyrecar Inc.'s motion to compel joinder is denied and its alternative motion to consolidate case numbers CGC-18-571257, Nathaniel Farber vs. Hyrecar Inc. and CGC-18-571258, Josiah Larkin vs. Hyrecar Inc., is granted.
The fact that the plaintiffs in both actions are parties to the same contract with defendant HyreCar does not require joinder of both plaintiffs, absent a showing that they are necessary parties for each other's actions under the statutory factors set forth in Code of Civil Procedure section 389(a). Here, complete relief between HyreCar and Mr. Farber and HyreCar and Mr. Larkin can be accorded without joining Mr. Farber and Mr. Larkin in a single action as co-plaintiffs. (Countrywide Home Loans, Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 785, 794 ["the term complete relief refers only 'to relief as between the persons already parties, and not as between a party and the absent person whose joinder is sought.'"]). While HyreCar may be exposed to inconsistent rulings on its contractual obligations if the plaintiffs are not joined, HyreCar does not risk being unable to comply with its obligations towards plaintiffs or of suffering multiple liability. (See Van Zant v. Apple Inc. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 965, 977 ["'[I]inconsistent obligations occur when a party is unable to comply with one court's order without breaching another court's order concerning the same incident.' [Citation.] In contrast, inconsistent adjudications or results occur when a party wins on a claim in one forum and loses on another claim from the same incident in another forum.'"].)

It is undisputed, however, that the two actions involve almost identical questions of law and fact and would result in unnecessary inefficiencies and create a risk of inconsistent adjudication if ligated separately. (See In re Marriage of Davis (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 294, 304 ["The law abhors multiplicity of actions, consuming the time of the court and entailing additional expense to the people."]). There is no danger that consolidating the actions will result in confusion of the jury. Any concern regarding prejudice resulting from the introduction of evidence of Mr. Larkin's criminal conviction may be addressed in the trial court through motions in limine. Therefore, the cases are consolidated for all purposes with CGC-18-571257 as the lead case; however, separate verdicts and judgments shall be entered as to each plaintiff.

Counsel for HyreCar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Set For Trial On May-11-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
